retrieved_evidence.text: OKLAHOMA CITY — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored 30 points as the Thunder beat the Knicks 111-100 on Sunday, March 29. Jalen Brunson led New York with 32, AP reports. #Knicks
OKLAHOMA CITY — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored 30 points as the Thunder pulled away late to beat the Knicks 111-100 on Sunday night at Paycom Center, per AP. #Knicks

The matchup between the Oklahoma City Thunder and the New York Knicks was held on Sunday, March 29, 2026, according to the schedule information released by the league office and reflected in team communications. The evening tipoff drew several thousand spectators and was part of a broader slate of games across the association that day. Officials said the date fell during a compressed stretch of the regular season, with both franchises navigating demanding travel itineraries and limited recovery time between contests.

In Oklahoma City on March 29, the matchup between the Oklahoma City Thunder and the New York Knicks in the NBA regular season highlighted Shai Gilgeous-Alexander’s growing status as a premier guard. According to game accounts, Gilgeous-Alexander repeatedly attacked the rim and created separation off the dribble, combining smooth drives, pull-up jumpers and trips to the free-throw line on his way to 30 points in the 111-100 Thunder victory. Observers noted his confident celebrations with teammates after key baskets in the second half, underscoring his role as the focal point of Oklahoma City’s offense in the home win.

Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander contributed 30 points in the recent home win over the New York Knicks, according to preliminary box-score figures. Team staff noted that he scored from a mix of drives to the basket and mid-range attempts, while also drawing multiple fouls that led to free throws. Coaches said his offensive output, combined with secondary playmaking and late-game composure, played a central role in maintaining the team’s advantage as the contest moved into its decisive stages.
OKLAHOMA CITY — The Knicks fell 111-100 to the Thunder on March 29 as OKC closed strong in the fourth quarter, according to AP game coverage carried by CBS and ABC. New York heads to Houston next.

Jalen Brunson’s performance for the New York Knicks on March 29 in Oklahoma City demonstrated his importance to New York’s backcourt, even as the team ultimately lost 111-100 to the Thunder. In recent reports on the contest, Brunson is described handling the ball extensively in a Knicks uniform, probing the defense with hesitations and step-backs, and scoring from both midrange and beyond the arc while guarded by Oklahoma City defenders. He finished with 32 points to lead New York in scoring, but despite his efforts the Thunder’s late surge and efficient closing stretch, anchored by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, secured the home side’s win.

New York guard Jalen Brunson led the Knicks with 32 points in their recent road loss to the Oklahoma City Thunder, early statistical reports indicated. According to team personnel, Brunson carried a significant share of the scoring load, finding opportunities in half-court sets and occasional transition sequences. Coaching staff acknowledged his efficiency and ability to create shots under pressure, while adding that the team struggled at times to generate consistent offensive support around him over the course of the game.

Observers at the recent meeting between the Thunder and the Knicks reported that Oklahoma City created decisive separation in the fourth quarter, after a largely competitive game through three periods. According to coaches and players, adjustments on the defensive end and improved shot selection contributed to a late run that extended the margin into double digits. The Knicks were said to have encountered difficulty matching the home side’s pace and execution in the final minutes, despite remaining within reach for much of the night.

The Oklahoma City Thunder recorded a 111-100 home victory over the New York Knicks in a recent regular-season matchup, according to league summaries. The contest, played before a partisan crowd in central Oklahoma, saw the hosts maintain a narrow advantage for much of the night before sealing the result in the closing stages. Team officials described the win as an important test against an Eastern Conference opponent, noting that both clubs had entered the game with comparable records and playoff aspirations, while further statistical breakdowns were still being compiled by the league.

The Thunder’s home contest against the New York Knicks was played in the team’s usual downtown arena, according to arena operations staff and information from local authorities. The facility, which regularly hosts the franchise’s regular-season schedule, accommodated a crowd that league officials described as near the typical attendance for similar games. Security, ticketing and broadcasting arrangements followed standard procedures for major professional events in the city, with local services reporting no significant disruptions around the venue.

Following the road game in Oklahoma City, the Knicks were expected to continue their regular-season schedule with another matchup in the coming days, according to team planning discussions. Club officials emphasized that any reference to a subsequent opponent, including a possible meeting with Houston, would be based on the official league schedule and internal confirmations before being circulated publicly. Staff members noted that travel and preparation plans would be adjusted in line with the confirmed calendar as the season progressed.
